_**User**_

Watchlist Screen
Reference Designs:  https://github.com/NaveenSinghTTN/ai-first-dev-training-mobile/blob/main/phase-2/docs/design/screens/watchlist.png  https://github.com/NaveenSinghTTN/ai-first-dev-training-mobile/blob/main/phase-2/docs/design/screens/watchlist_empty.png 

State 1 — Populated
Header

"YOUR COLLECTION" label: label-sm, on_surface_variant, uppercase, tracked
"My Watchlist" title: display-md Manrope
Search icon + circular user avatar top-right
Filter Chips

All | Movies | Series
Active: secondary_container background
Filters the displayed list client-side — no re-fetch
"All" shows everything; "Movies" shows only movies; "Series" shows only TV shows
Content Grid

2-column grid of content cards
Each card:
Portrait poster image (2:3 ratio), 16px radius
Rating badge: top-right corner, surface_container_highest bg, primary_container star, label-sm value
"×" remove icon: top-right of card area (below badge or overlaid)
Title: title-lg, on_surface, truncated with ellipsis
Year • Genre(s): label-sm, on_surface_variant
"Details" button: full-width, surface_container_highest bg, on_surface text, title-sm, 8px radius
"Because you saved [Title]" Row

Appears only when watchlist has at least one item
Based on the most recently added item
"See All" right-aligned in primary_container
Horizontal scroll of portrait content cards, sourced from /movie/{id}/similar for that title
If Similar returns empty, hide this section
Empty State Within a Filter

If the user selects "Series" but has only saved movies, show a contextual empty message within the filtered view: "No [Series] in your watchlist yet" with a "Browse All" chip that resets to "All" filter.
State 2 — Empty (Zero Items in Watchlist)
"YOUR COLLECTION" label + "My Watchlist" title + "0 titles" in on_surface_variant
Large bookmark icon centred, secondary_container colour
"Your watchlist is empty" heading: headline-md
"Save movies and shows you want to watch later and they'll appear here": body-md, on_surface_variant, centred
"Browse Trending Now" CTA: primary gradient button, navigates to Home tab
"POPULAR RECOMMENDATIONS" section: label-sm uppercase heading in on_surface_variant, followed by trending content (optional to load — skeleton cards are acceptable)
Interactions
Tapping "×" removes item with optimistic UI: item disappears immediately from grid. If AsyncStorage write fails, snap back and show a brief error toast.
Tapping "Details" navigates to DetailScreen
Tab bar Watchlist icon shows a numeric badge when count > 0, using primary_container colour
Badge updates reactively as items are added/removed from any screen
Minimum Bar
Both populated and empty states displaying correctly. Remove action working and persisted. Navigation to Detail working.

What Good Looks Like
Optimistic removal with snap-back on failure. All three filter chips working. "Because you saved" row loading correctly. Badge on tab updating reactively. Correct contextual empty state when a filter returns no results in a non-empty watchlist.



---

**Handling optimistic updates**

I'm implementing custom persist storage with try/catch to handle async failures during optimistic removal. I'm adjusting the optimistic update strategy to only apply to UI updates, not the actual persistence, and will implement a remove method with flushSync-like pattern. I'm implementing error handling for storage operations by wrapping `createJSONStorage` to catch setItem errors and notify with old state.

I'm adding a custom async storage wrapper to handle AsyncStorage persistence delays in the watchlist store. I'm implementing a two-step remove process in the hook using `getState().items` snapshot for rollback. 

I'm implementing rollback using a snapshot before `set` and `AsyncStorage.setItem` with the persisted partialized state. I'm replacing remove with a skipPersist flag to prevent double writes during optimistic updates. I'm implementing remove with try/catch around AsyncStorage.setItem using the same key 'streamlist-watchlist'. I'm designing the schema for `media_type` field while noting TV pipeline work is deferred for now. I'm checking React Navigation 7 API for `tabBarBadgeStyle` and `letterSpacing` implementations. I'm removing persist middleware to avoid race conditions during async AsyncStorage operations. Parameters:

```json
{
  "name": "Watchlist Screen Spec",
  "overview": "Replace the minimal Watchlist screen with the full two-state design: populated grid with filters, “Because you saved” similar row, optimistic removal with persist rollback, empty state with trending recommendations, reactive tab badge, and enriched persisted items for year/genre display—all while keeping data fetching in hooks and aligning with existing theme/navigation patterns.",
  "plan": "# Watchlist screen implementation plan\n\n## Current state\n\n- [&#96;src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx&#96;](src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx): simple title + &#96;FlatList&#96; + basic &#96;ContentCard&#96;; no header, filters, badge, or empty-state design.\n- [&#96;src/store/watchlistStore.ts&#96;](src/store/watchlistStore.ts): Zustand + &#96;persist&#96; with &#96;MovieSummary[]&#96;; append-only add; no &#96;savedAt&#96;, &#96;media_type&#96;, or year/genre snapshots.\n- [&#96;src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx&#96;](src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx): &#96;WatchlistTab&#96; has no &#96;tabBarBadge&#96;; tab bar is defined in one component (good place to inject reactive badge via a child wrapper—see below).\n- [&#96;src/api/movies.ts&#96;](src/api/movies.ts): &#96;fetchSimilarMoviesPage&#96; and &#96;fetchTrendingMoviesPage&#96; already exist for “Because you saved” and “Popular recommendations.”\n- [&#96;src/components/common/ContentCard.tsx&#96;](src/components/common/ContentCard.tsx): horizontal list card (120×180, &#96;spacing.sm&#96; radius)—**not** sufficient for the watchlist grid spec (16px radius, rating badge, remove, Details CTA, year • genres). Prefer a **dedicated** watchlist grid component rather than overloading &#96;ContentCard&#96; (keeps home/detail rows unchanged).\n\n**Important constraint:** There is **no TV API or &#96;media_type&#96; in the app today**; all saves come from movie [&#96;DetailScreen&#96;](src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx). To satisfy “All | Movies | Series” and contextual empty copy, persist **&#96;media_type: 'movie' | 'tv'&#96;** (default **&#96;movie&#96;** for migration and all current flows). **Series** will only show items once a future TV save path sets &#96;media_type: 'tv'&#96;; until then, Series behaves as an **empty filter** with the correct “No Series in your watchlist yet” + “Browse All” chip—still “working” per spec.\n\n---\n\n## Architecture (per project rules)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart LR\n  subgraph hooks [src/hooks]\n    useWatchlistScreen[\"useWatchlistScreen or split hooks\"]\n    useWatchlistSimilar[\"useWatchlistSimilar\"]\n    useWatchlistTrending[\"useWatchlistTrendingPreview\"]\n  end\n  subgraph screen [WatchlistScreen]\n    UI[\"Layout + presentational components only\"]\n  end\n  subgraph store [watchlistStore]\n    Zustand[\"items + hydrated + remove with rollback\"]\n  end\n  UI --\u003e useWatchlistScreen\n  useWatchlistSimilar --\u003e movies[\"api/movies.ts\"]\n  useWatchlistTrending --\u003e movies\n  useWatchlistScreen --\u003e Zustand\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- **Screens:** no &#96;useEffect&#96; + API; compose UI and call hooks only.\n- **API:** only through [&#96;src/api/client.ts&#96;](src/api/client.ts) via functions in [&#96;src/api/movies.ts&#96;](src/api/movies.ts) (already true).\n- **New hooks (suggested split):**\n  - **&#96;useWatchlistSimilarRow&#96;**: input = &#96;anchorMovieId: string | null&#96; (derived from store: item with **max &#96;savedAt&#96;** among non-empty items). Calls &#96;fetchSimilarMoviesPage(anchorMovieId, 1)&#96; + &#96;mapMoviesPage&#96;; loading/error/empty handled (hide section if empty or error after load—spec: hide if similar empty).\n  - **&#96;useWatchlistTrendingPreview&#96;**: page 1 &#96;fetchTrendingMoviesPage&#96; for empty-state horizontal list; skeleton until resolved (per spec).\n  - **&#96;useWatchlistFilter&#96;**: local state &#96;All | Movies | Series&#96;; **client-side** &#96;useMemo&#96; filter over &#96;items&#96; (no refetch). Optional: single composed hook **&#96;useWatchlistScreenModel&#96;** that returns filtered list + chip state + derived anchor id to keep the screen thin.\n\n---\n\n## Data model and store changes\n\n**New type** (e.g. &#96;WatchlistItem&#96; in [&#96;src/api/types.ts&#96;](src/api/types.ts)) extending &#96;MovieSummary&#96; with:\n\n| Field | Purpose |\n|--------|--------|\n| &#96;media_type&#96; | &#96;'movie' \\| 'tv'&#96; — default &#96;'movie'&#96; |\n| &#96;release_date&#96; | &#96;string \\| null&#96; — year from first 4 chars for display |\n| &#96;genre_names&#96; | &#96;string[]&#96; — snapshot at save time (first 1–2 for subtitle) |\n| &#96;savedAt&#96; | &#96;number&#96; (ms) — **order “most recently added”** for “Because you saved” |\n\n**Migration:** On rehydrate, map legacy items missing these fields: &#96;media_type: 'movie'&#96;, &#96;savedAt: index&#96; or &#96;Date.now()&#96; (pick one deterministic rule—e.g. **preserve array order** and assign monotonic &#96;savedAt&#96; so “last in list ≈ most recent** only if** we also update &#96;add&#96;/&#96;toggle&#96; to append and set &#96;savedAt: Date.now()&#96; going forward). Prefer **&#96;savedAt: Date.now()&#96;** on every add/toggle-on so “most recent” is correct even after re-add.\n\n**&#96;toSummary&#96; / &#96;toggle&#96; / &#96;add&#96;:** When saving from [&#96;DetailScreen&#96;](src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx), pass **&#96;MovieDetail&#96;** so the store can fill &#96;release_date&#96;, &#96;genre_names&#96; (from &#96;genres.map(g =\u003e g.name)&#96;), &#96;media_type: 'movie'&#96;.\n\n**Remove + optimistic UI + persist failure:** Today &#96;persist&#96; writes asynchronously after &#96;set&#96;. Plan one of these (pick smallest that works):\n\n1. **Custom JSON storage wrapper** for &#96;createJSONStorage&#96;: before &#96;remove&#96;, set a module-level &#96;rollback = () =\u003e setState({ items: prev })&#96;; on &#96;setItem&#96; **failure**, call &#96;rollback&#96; and show toast; clear &#96;rollback&#96; after success. Serialize rapid removes (e.g. ignore overlapping rollbacks or queue) to avoid clobbering &#96;rollback&#96;.\n2. If the wrapper is too fragile, **documented fallback:** &#96;removeAsync&#96; that snapshots → optimistic &#96;set&#96; → &#96;await AsyncStorage.setItem&#96; with the **same key/shape** as zustand persist (duplication risk—only if (1) fails).\n\nAdd **&#96;showWatchlistErrorToast()&#96;** (or reuse pattern) in [&#96;src/utils/toast.ts&#96;](src/utils/toast.ts) using existing [&#96;toastConfig&#96;](src/theme/toastConfig.tsx) (&#96;Toast.show&#96;).\n\n---\n\n## UI building blocks (new files under &#96;src/components/watchlist/&#96;)\n\nKeep styles in &#96;StyleSheet.create&#96;; colors/spacing/typography from theme only.\n\n1. **&#96;WatchlistHeader&#96;**: “YOUR COLLECTION” (&#96;label-sm&#96; + uppercase + **tracked**—add a small typography variant in [&#96;src/theme/typography.ts&#96;](src/theme/typography.ts) e.g. &#96;letterSpacing&#96; for the label only, &#96;on_surface_variant&#96;), “My Watchlist” (&#96;display-md&#96;, &#96;on_surface&#96;), row with search &#96;Pressable&#96; → &#96;navigation.navigate('SearchTab')&#96;, avatar &#96;Pressable&#96; → &#96;navigation.navigate('ProfileTab')&#96; (matches existing Profile “coming soon” behavior). Safe area via &#96;useSafeAreaInsets&#96;.\n\n2. **&#96;WatchlistFilterChips&#96;**: Three chips—All / Movies / Series. Active: &#96;backgroundColor: colors.secondary_container&#96;. Inactive: neutral surface/outline per existing chip patterns ([&#96;GenreFilterStrip&#96;](src/components/home/GenreFilterStrip.tsx) is a reference; labels differ: “Movies” not genre names).\n\n3. **&#96;WatchlistGridCard&#96;**: Column width from &#96;useWindowDimensions&#96; (padding + gap): **2 columns**, poster **2:3**, **&#96;borderRadius: spacing.md&#96; (16px)**, rating badge (top-right: &#96;surface_container_highest&#96; bg, star in &#96;primary_container&#96;, &#96;label-sm&#96; value), remove “×” (top area—&#96;Pressable&#96; with &#96;hitSlop&#96;; z-index above image), title &#96;title-lg&#96; truncated, subtitle “Year • Genres” &#96;label-sm&#96; &#96;on_surface_variant&#96;, full-width **Details** button (&#96;surface_container_highest&#96;, &#96;title-sm&#96;, &#96;borderRadius: spacing.sm&#96; (8px)). Details + card tap area: Details navigates to Detail; remove only removes.\n\n4. **&#96;BecauseYouSavedRow&#96;**: Only if &#96;items.length \u003e 0&#96;; title **“Because you saved [Title]”** (truncate title); **“See All”** right-aligned &#96;primary_container&#96; → navigate &#96;HomeTab&#96; → &#96;SeeAll&#96; with &#96;listType: 'similar'&#96;, &#96;movieId&#96;, &#96;title&#96; (already in [&#96;HomeStackParamList&#96;](src/navigation/types.ts)). Horizontal &#96;ScrollView&#96; of cards (reuse small poster + tap to Detail—either slim wrapper around shared poster sizing or compact card consistent with layout tokens). **Hide** if similar hook returns **empty** after success.\n\n5. **&#96;WatchlistFilterEmpty&#96;** (contextual): When filter active but filtered list empty **and** raw &#96;items.length \u003e 0&#96;: message **“No [Series|Movies] in your watchlist yet”** + chip **“Browse All”** → sets filter to All.\n\n6. **&#96;WatchlistEmptyState&#96;**: “YOUR COLLECTION” + “My Watchlist” + **“0 titles”** (&#96;on_surface_variant&#96;); centered bookmark icon (&#96;secondary_container&#96;); headline + body per spec; **“Browse Trending Now”** CTA: &#96;LinearGradient&#96; like [&#96;DetailWatchlistButton&#96;](src/components/detail/DetailWatchlistButton.tsx) → &#96;navigation.navigate('HomeTab', { screen: 'HomeMain' })&#96;; **“POPULAR RECOMMENDATIONS”** + horizontal trending (skeletons while loading).\n\n**List implementation:** &#96;FlatList&#96; with &#96;numColumns={2}&#96;, &#96;columnWrapperStyle&#96; for row gap, &#96;ListHeaderComponent&#96; = header + chips + (optional) Because row + spacer; &#96;ListEmptyComponent&#96; = either full empty state (zero items) or filter empty inline; bottom padding &#96;insets.bottom + layout.tabBarStackPadding&#96;. Wait for **&#96;hydrated&#96;** before showing main list (same pattern as [&#96;DetailScreen&#96;](src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx) watchlist button).\n\n---\n\n## Navigation\n\n- **Detail:** &#96;navigation.navigate('HomeTab', { screen: 'Detail', params: { movieId } })&#96; (existing pattern in current watchlist).\n- **Reactive tab badge:** Extract the tab navigator into an inner component (e.g. &#96;MainTabs&#96;) inside [&#96;RootNavigator.tsx&#96;](src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx) that calls &#96;useWatchlistStore((s) =\u003e s.items.length)&#96; and passes **&#96;tabBarBadge&#96;** / **&#96;tabBarBadgeStyle&#96;** (&#96;backgroundColor: colors.primary_container&#96;) on &#96;WatchlistTab&#96; when &#96;count \u003e 0&#96; (and optionally hide until &#96;hydrated&#96; to avoid flicker). Re-renders when store updates from any screen.\n\n---\n\n## Theme / layout tokens\n\n- Add **&#96;watchlistGridGap&#96;** (or reuse &#96;spacing.md&#96;) in [&#96;src/theme/layout.ts&#96;](src/theme/layout.ts) if helpful for column math.\n- Poster width for grid: &#96;(width - horizontalPadding*2 - gap) / 2&#96;; height &#96;width * 3/2&#96;.\n- Any new typography (tracked label) lives in [&#96;src/theme/typography.ts&#96;](src/theme/typography.ts)—no inline font objects.\n\n---\n\n## Testing\n\n- Extend or add tests for store migration / filter helpers if extracted (pure functions).\n- [&#96;__tests__/App.test.tsx&#96;](__tests__/App.test.tsx) stays mocked at navigator level; no change required unless you add a dedicated watchlist store test file (recommended for &#96;savedAt&#96; / filter logic).\n\n---\n\n## Reference designs\n\nUse [watchlist.png](https://github.com/NaveenSinghTTN/ai-first-dev-training-mobile/blob/main/phase-2/docs/design/screens/watchlist.png) and [watchlist_empty.png](https://github.com/NaveenSinghTTN/ai-first-dev-training-mobile/blob/main/phase-2/docs/design/screens/watchlist_empty.png) for spacing/visual parity during implementation.\n\n---\n\n## Acceptance checklist (from your spec)\n\n| Requirement | Approach |\n|-------------|----------|\n| Populated + empty states | Two branches in &#96;WatchlistScreen&#96; + subcomponents |\n| Filters client-side | &#96;useMemo&#96; + &#96;media_type&#96; |\n| 2-col grid + card chrome | &#96;WatchlistGridCard&#96; + measured width |\n| Because you saved + similar API | Hook + anchor by &#96;savedAt&#96; |\n| Similar empty → hide row | Conditional render on results length |\n| Filter empty (non-empty list) | &#96;WatchlistFilterEmpty&#96; |\n| Optimistic remove + rollback | Custom storage or async persist + toast |\n| Details navigation | Same stack params as today |\n| Tab badge reactive | &#96;MainTabs&#96; + store subscription |\n| Minimum bar | Hydration gate, remove persisted, Detail navigation |\n",
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Adding watchlist item type**

I'm defining WatchlistItem type with media_type, release_date, genre_names, and savedAt fields. 

**Implementing persist middleware**

I'm implementing custom storage wrapper with rollback ref for zustand persist middleware. I'm using `onRehydrateStorage` callback to handle persisted state before merge, leveraging `partialize` to manage incoming storage state. I'm implementing a custom `merge` function to handle state reconciliation during rehydration with zustand 5's new API. 

I'm implementing a custom merge function for state reconciliation during rehydration with zustand 5's new API. 

**Handling storage rehydration**

I'm normalizing state during rehydration using `onRehydrateStorage` to ensure item migrations are applied consistently. I'm implementing item migration in `normalizeWatchlistItems` called from the rehydrate callback. I'm adding a custom merge function to properly handle legacy item migration during rehydration. 

**Adding util functions**

I'm creating a utility file for watchlist item normalization. I'm implementing watchlist item normalization with proper savedAt timestamps using index-based assignment. I'm normalizing watchlist items by setting savedAt timestamps based on legacy index order, where the last item in array gets the highest timestamp for sorting purposes. I'm implementing timestamp normalization for legacy watchlist migration using `Date.now() - (length - 1 - idx) * 1000` to ensure proper ordering. I'm implementing rollback logic for watchlist migration with proper error handling and cleanup. 

**Handling rollback logic**

I'm refactoring the rollback mechanism to use a generation counter to handle concurrent remove operations properly. I'm implementing a rollback stack to handle concurrent remove operations properly. I'm simplifying rollback handling by using synchronous operations and manual persistence instead of complex LIFO logic. 

I'm fixing the rollback logic to correctly handle nested operations and ensure proper state restoration. I'm implementing a rollback stack to serialize setItem operations and restore the proper mutation chain. I'm simplifying rollback handling to use a single pendingRollback instead of a stack, documenting rapid concurrent operations as a limitation. I'm implementing a rollback stack using an array to track removes and popping on setItem success, ensuring one-to-one matching with zustand's setItem calls. I'm ensuring rollback order matches setItem execution order to handle asynchronous AsyncStorage operations correctly. </details></think>

I'm calculating card width with padding and gap adjustments using `cardWidth = (width - 2*padding - gap) / 2`. 

**Handling empty state**

I'm adjusting the watchlist empty state logic to show the trending preview when items are empty and hydrated. I'm implementing the logic to show 'Because you saved' header only when items exist, and using skeleton loading for empty states. </details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Fixing FlatList layout**

The columnWrapperStyle only applies when items are in pairs, causing layout issues with single items in a 2-column FlatList. I'm addressing the columnWrapperStyle gap issue in React Native 0.71+ by using margin on cards instead of gap for single items. I'm adjusting padding behavior for FlatList with numColumns by moving paddingHorizontal to contentContainerStyle for proper first-row spacing. 

**Adjusting FlatList layout**

I noticed columnWrapper padding applies to last row single items and adjusted the BecauseYouSavedRow hook to prevent idle status when anchorMovieId exists. I'm adjusting the initial state handling in `useWatchlistSimilarRow` to prevent flashing idle state before loading begins. The navigation to ProfileTab is blocked by tab press listeners that prevent default behavior. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

Here’s what was implemented and how it maps to the plan.

### Data & persistence
- **[`src/api/types.ts`](src/api/types.ts)** — Added `WatchlistMediaType` and `WatchlistItem` (`media_type`, `release_date`, `genre_names`, `savedAt`).
- **[`src/utils/watchlistItem.ts`](src/utils/watchlistItem.ts)** — `toWatchlistItem`, `normalizeWatchlistItem` (migration), `filterWatchlistItems`, `anchorItemForBecauseYouSaved`, `releaseYear`, `formatGenreSubtitle`.
- **[`src/store/watchlistStore.ts`](src/store/watchlistStore.ts)** — Items are `WatchlistItem[]`; add/toggle use `toWatchlistItem` from detail/list; **rollback-aware `StateStorage`** wraps AsyncStorage so a failed `setItem` runs the pending rollback and [`showWatchlistErrorToast`](src/utils/toast.ts); **custom `merge`** normalizes legacy rows.
- **[`src/utils/toast.ts`](src/utils/toast.ts)** — `showWatchlistErrorToast`.

### Hooks (no fetching in the screen)
- **[`src/hooks/useWatchlistSimilarRow.ts`](src/hooks/useWatchlistSimilarRow.ts)** — `/movie/{id}/similar` page 1.
- **[`src/hooks/useWatchlistTrendingPreview.ts`](src/hooks/useWatchlistTrendingPreview.ts)** — Trending for the empty state (gated by `enabled`).
- **[`src/hooks/useWatchlistScreenModel.ts`](src/hooks/useWatchlistScreenModel.ts)** — Filter state + filtered list + anchor for “Because you saved”.

### UI ([`src/components/watchlist/`](src/components/watchlist/))
- **`WatchlistHeader`** — Label + “My Watchlist”, search → `SearchTab`, profile icon → `ProfileTab`.
- **`WatchlistFilterChips`** — All / Movies / Series with `secondary_container` when active.
- **`WatchlistGridCard`** — 2:3 poster (`spacing.md` radius), rating pill, × remove, title, year • genres, full-width Details.
- **`BecauseYouSavedRow`** — “Because you saved …”, See All → `SeeAll` similar, horizontal cards; loading/idle uses `DetailSimilarSkeleton`; hidden on error or empty results.
- **`WatchlistFilterEmpty`** — Filtered empty copy + “Browse All”.
- **`WatchlistEmptyState`** — 0 titles, bookmark, CTA gradient → `HomeMain`, “POPULAR RECOMMENDATIONS” + trending or skeletons.

### Screen & navigation
- **[`src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx`](src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx)** — Hydration skeleton, empty vs populated `FlatList` (`numColumns={2}`), header + chips + because row in `ListHeaderComponent`, navigation to Detail / See All / Home / Search / Profile.
- **[`src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx`](src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx)** — **`MainTabs`** reads watchlist count + hydration and sets **`tabBarBadge`** / **`tabBarBadgeStyle`** on Watchlist.

### Theme
- **[`src/theme/typography.ts`](src/theme/typography.ts)** — `label-sm-tracked` for uppercase labels.
- **[`src/theme/layout.ts`](src/theme/layout.ts)** — `watchlistGridGap`.

### Tests
- **[`__tests__/watchlistItem.test.ts`](__tests__/watchlistItem.test.ts)** — Migration defaulting, filters, anchor by `savedAt`.

**Note:** Everything saved from the current movie detail flow is `media_type: 'movie'`. **Series** is supported in the model and filters; it stays empty until a TV save path sets `media_type: 'tv'`, with the contextual empty state + “Browse All” as specified.

All Jest tests pass (`yarn test`).

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

Here’s what was wrong and what we changed.

### What was wrong
1. **`ItemSeparatorComponent` + `numColumns > 1`** — In React Native, separators don’t map to “rows” in a multi-column list; they sit between **every** pair of items, so spacing didn’t match a real grid.
2. **Fixed 120px cards** — Three fixed-width `ContentCard`s didn’t use the row width, so columns looked uneven on different screens.
3. **Spinners** — Initial and “load more” states used `ActivityIndicator` instead of skeletons.

### What we did

**[`src/components/common/ContentCard.tsx`](src/components/common/ContentCard.tsx)**  
- Added optional **`posterWidth`**. When set, card and poster use that width and a **2:3** height (`height = round(width × 3/2)`). Defaults stay `layout.contentPosterWidth` / `contentPosterHeight` behavior.

**[`src/screens/SeeAllScreen.tsx`](src/screens/SeeAllScreen.tsx)**  
- Uses **`useWindowDimensions`** with horizontal padding `spacing.md` and **`layout.watchlistGridGap`** between columns.  
- **Column width:**  
  `(screenWidth - 2×padding - 2×gaps) / 3` so three columns + two gaps fill the row.  
- **`columnWrapperStyle`** on the `FlatList`: `flexDirection: 'row'`, **`gap`** (column gap), **`marginBottom`** (row gap).  
- **`ItemSeparatorComponent` removed.**  
- **Initial load:** full **`SeeAllGridSkeleton`** (4×3 poster placeholders).  
- **Load more:** **`SeeAllFooterSkeleton`** — label + three skeleton tiles (no spinner).

**[`src/components/seeAll/SeeAllSkeletons.tsx`](src/components/seeAll/SeeAllSkeletons.tsx)** (new)  
- `SeeAllGridSkeleton` and `SeeAllFooterSkeleton` using theme colors (`surface_container`) and typography for the footer label.

Typecheck and tests are green. If anything still looks off on a specific device, say which one and we can tune padding/gaps.

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

Here’s what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Cause
1. **Vector icons** – On **iOS**, `MaterialCommunityIcons.ttf` must be listed under **`UIAppFonts`** in `Info.plist` or icons render as empty boxes. On **Android**, the icon font wasn’t being copied into assets until **`fonts.gradle`** from `react-native-vector-icons` was applied.
2. **See All back** – Stack `headerTintColor` was `primary_container` (coral), not white.
3. **Tab bar** – Opaque/absolute bar without safe-area padding and equal flex on items made tabs easy to clip or crowd on small screens; Android also benefited from a solid bar background so icons stay visible.

### Changes

| Area | Change |
|------|--------|
| **[`src/theme/colors.ts`](src/theme/colors.ts)** | Added **`icon_on_dark`** (`#FFFFFF`) for header/nav icons. |
| **[`ios/StreamList/Info.plist`](ios/StreamList/Info.plist)** | Added **`UIAppFonts`** → `MaterialCommunityIcons.ttf`. |
| **[`android/app/build.gradle`](android/app/build.gradle)** | **`project.ext.vectoricons`** + **`apply from: .../fonts.gradle`** so the font is bundled on Android. |
| **[`src/screens/SeeAllScreen.tsx`](src/screens/SeeAllScreen.tsx)** | **`useLayoutEffect`** sets a custom **`headerLeft`** with **`MaterialCommunityIcons`** `arrow-left` using **`colors.icon_on_dark`**, and **`headerBackVisible: false`** so the default back control doesn’t duplicate. |
| **[`src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx`](src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx)** | See All stack options use **`headerTintColor: colors.icon_on_dark`** (for any remaining native chrome). **`MainTabs`** uses **`useSafeAreaInsets`**, **`tabBarStyleForPlatform`** (Android: solid **`surface`** + **`elevation`**, iOS: transparent + blur), **`tabBarItemStyle`**: **`flex: 1`**, **`minWidth: 0`**, padding. Tab icons use **`layout.iconSizeTab` (26)**. |
| **[`src/theme/layout.ts`](src/theme/layout.ts)** | Added **`iconSizeTab: 26`** for bottom tab icons. |

### What you should run locally
- **Android:** Clean/rebuild so `fonts.gradle` runs, e.g. `cd android && ./gradlew clean` then run the app again.
- **iOS:** `cd ios && pod install`, then rebuild (so `Info.plist` + pods are picked up).

After a full rebuild, Material icons should render in the tab bar and elsewhere, the See All back control should stay **white**, and the tab bar should lay out with safe-area insets and clearer Android contrast.

Here’s what was updated to match the reference hero and tighten home alignment.

### Hero banner ([`src/components/home/HeroFeaturedCard.tsx`](src/components/home/HeroFeaturedCard.tsx))
- **Layout:** Uses **`useWindowDimensions`** so width is `screen − 2×16px` (aligned with the genre strip). **Portrait 2:3** height via `layout.heroFeaturedAspectW/H` (replacing 16:9).
- **Image:** **`resizeMode="cover"`** on `ImageBackground`; **16px** corner radius (`spacing.md`).
- **Gradient:** Three stops (`surface_clear` → `hero_gradient_mid` → `hero_gradient_bottom`) so the lower area reads clearly.
- **“NEW RELEASE”:** Salmon **`hero_badge_background`**, **bold** uppercase label in **`hero_on_salmon`**, **4px** radius (`spacing.xs`).
- **Title:** **`icon_on_dark`**, **uppercase**, Manrope **`display-md`**, **12px** under badge (`spacing.md_sm`), **12px** above synopsis.
- **Synopsis:** **`body-md`**, **`on_surface_variant`**, **24px** above buttons (`spacing.lg`).
- **Buttons:** **Watch Now** = solid salmon (no red gradient), **play** icon + label in **`hero_on_salmon`**, **`flex: 3`**. **Details** = **`hero_secondary_overlay`** + hairline border, white label, **`flex: 2`**. **12px** gap between them (`spacing.md_sm`), **16px** corner radius.
- **Inner padding:** Horizontal **16px**, bottom **24px**, extra **top** on the text block so content sits in the **lower third**.

### Theme ([`src/theme/colors.ts`](src/theme/colors.ts), [`src/theme/spacing.ts`](src/theme/spacing.ts), [`src/theme/layout.ts`](src/theme/layout.ts))
- New: **`hero_badge_background`**, **`hero_on_salmon`**, **`hero_secondary_overlay`**, **`hero_gradient_mid`**, **`hero_gradient_bottom`**.
- New spacing: **`md_sm` (12)** for the spec gaps.
- New layout: **`heroFeaturedAspectW/H`** (2:3 portrait).

### Home screen ([`src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx`](src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx))
- **`heroBlock`:** **`alignItems: 'center'`**, **`marginTop: spacing.md_sm`** for spacing under the chips.
- **`filterSection`:** **`paddingBottom: spacing.sm`** so chip row ↔ hero spacing matches the design rhythm.

Rebuild the app to review on device; the featured card should now follow the salmon pill, white title, frosted Details, and spacing called out in your reference.
